{
  "most_likely_causes": [

    "Precision decline with relatively stable recall",

    "Introduction of a new payment channel",
    "Fraud patterns changed after a promotional campaign",
    "Feature distribution for transaction_amount shifted significantly"
  ],
  "less_likely_causes": [
    "threshold miscalibration",
    "pipeline failure"
  ],
  "recommended_diagnostics": [
    "Analysis of feature distribution shifts for all features",
    "Comparison of fraud patterns before and after the promotional campaign",
    "Examination of transaction data from the new payment channel",
    "Evaluation of model performance on a held-out test set",
    "Monitoring of model metrics over time to detect further degradation"
  ],
  "short_term_actions": [
    "Investigate and address potential issues with the new payment channel",
    "Update model thresholds to mitigate precision decline"
  ],
  "long_term_actions": [
    "Retrain the model using updated data that reflects the new payment channel and changed fraud patterns",
    "Implement ongoing model monitoring and retraining schedule",
    "Consider using online learning or incremental learning techniques to adapt to changing data distributions"
  ],
  "reasoning_summary": "The decline in precision with relatively stable recall suggests that the model is experiencing data drift or concept drift. The introduction of a new payment channel and changes in fraud patterns after a promotional campaign may have contributed to this drift. The significant shift in feature distribution for transaction_amount also supports this conclusion. Pipeline failure is unlikely due to the absence of failed jobs in the data pipeline logs. Threshold miscalibration is possible but less likely given the other evidence. Recommended diagnostics and actions focus on understanding and addressing the data drift and concept drift, as well as implementing ongoing model monitoring and maintenance."
}

evidence: Precision decline with relatively stable recall
Introduction of a new payment channel

recommended_diagnostics: Analysis of feature distribution shifts for all features

parsed_output.reasoning_summary: The decline in precision with relatively stable recall suggests that the model is experiencing data drift or concept drift. The introduction of a new payment channel and changes in fraud patterns after a promotional campaign may have contributed to this drift. The significant shift in feature distribution for transaction_amount also supports this conclusion. Pipeline failure is unlikely due to the absence of failed jobs in the data pipeline logs. Threshold miscalibration is possible but less likely given the other evidence. Recommended diagnostics and actions focus on understanding and addressing the data drift and concept drift, as well as implementing ongoing model monitoring and maintenance.
